msItem.author: Ivo of Chartres
msItem.title: Sermons
msItem.note: Here without title or author's name. The first sermon is preceded by a list of all the twenty-one which correspond with Migne's Patrologia Latina, tom. clxii, col. 505, sermm. 1-21, except that no. 5 begins 'Qualiter ueteris ac noui', wanting the first part as printed, and that the place of no. 6 is taken by the Prologus in Decretum (clxi, 47: fol. 28v).
msItem.incipit: De sacramento baptismatis ... Quoniam populus ad fidem uocatus

summary.p: English, 12th century, end, inboard binding, date of binding as manuscript, with a ‘Romanesque’ structure (cf. Szirmai, pp. 140-142); the sewing supports laced through oblong tunnels in the spine edge of the oak boards. Covered in alum-tawed skin with compound chevron endbands worked in green, red and natural coloured thread. This is the first binding of the manuscript. It is a late example of a binding with a ‘Romanesque’ structure and does not have spine linings or tab endbands.

There are brown stains of unknown cause to fols. ii verso-1r; similar unexplained brown stains are also found in other Bodleian manuscripts with a Salisbury provenance: MS. Arch. Selden B. 26, MSS. Bodl. 392, 444, 516, and at least nine manuscripts still at Salisbury (information from Anne Dutton, Cathedral Librarian).
The bookbinding is described in the order of binding using the controlled vocabulary of the Language of Bindings Thesaurus (LoB).

binding.head: First binding, English, 12th century, end
binding.endleaves: Left: Separate endleaves. Originally two blank bifolia of parchment, now comprising a partially lifted pastedown (fol. i) originally conjoint with a free endleaf (fol. ii), a second bifolium of parchment has been excised leaving two unfoliated conjoint stubs between fols. i and ii.
Right: Integral endleaves. Fol. 62 a blank parchment pastedown conjoint with fol. 57.
binding.sewing: Sewn all-along as linked supported or herringbone sewing with undyed plain thread at five sewing stations on three raised split-strap sewing supports of alum-tawed skin (9 mm wide). Sewing stations (measured in mm from the head-edge at fols. 12v-13r): 22 (kettle), 50 (support 1), 132 (support 2), 214 (support 3), 241 (kettle), 267 (tail). There are no earlier sewing stations.
binding.boards: Wooden oak boards the size of the bookblock, 11-14 mm thick (including covering material), wood identified from grain pattern exposed at inner face of boards. External rounded profile (quadrant moulding) to the boards, with small canted spine-edge corners. The three sewing supports are laced through oblong tunnels in the spine edge of the boards into channels in a long lacing path with wooden wedges securing the sewing supports. The left board is detached.
binding.edges: Bookblock edges cut flush and undecorated. The trim was minimal and many of the leaves preserve the uneven natural cut edges of the parchment skins. The bookblock has small canted spine-edge corners to accommodate the endbands.
binding.spine: The binding has a flat spine shape. No spine lining.
binding.endbands: Compound chevron endbands. Primary sewing on a single core of alum-tawed skin with plain undyed thread, the endband cores laced into the boards with a long lacing path. Secondary chevron sewing in green, red and natural plain thread (compact chevron on single core as secondary).
binding.covering: Full cover of alum-tawed skin used hairside out (from follicle pattern probably sheep), now a cream colour. The cover is not moulded over the spine or sewing supports and is turned in on all edges with lapped mitre and mitre corners. The turn-ins are irregular and have not been trimmed. The cover is now split at the left joint and there are losses at the head and tail of the spine. It is not clear whether the covering skin once projected as thin single-layer tabs, was trimmed to the height of the endbands, or was treated in another way.
binding.fastenings: None. There is an unused rectangular recess for a strap at the centre of the fore-edge of the left board beneath the covering.
binding.alterations: A 16th-century(?) annotation to fol. 29v copying the text on a scriptorium patch into the margin might indicate that the patch was once loose and has since been restuck.
binding.bookmarks: Loose blank paper bookmark between fols. 56v-57r.
binding.chaining: There are two holes at the tail fore-edge of the left board from a now lost Bodleian chain shackle or staple.
binding.labels: One line ink inscription to the head of right cover.

history.provenance: Salisbury, Wiltshire, Cathedral church of St Mary the Virgin; see removed from Sherborne in 1078: 'Liber ecclesie cathedralis Sarum' (s. xv/xvi). (MLGB3: evidence from an ex-libris inscription or note of gift to an institution).
history.acquisition: Apparently acquired by the Library between 1605 and 1611.

recordHist.source: Description adapted (2024) from the Summary Catalogue (1922). Decoration, localization and date follow Pächt and Alexander (1973). Summary Catalogue, vol. 2, part 1, p. 297

ref.title: MLGB3: Medieval Libraries of Great Britain

revisionDesc.change: Description revised to incorporate all information from Summary Catalogue.
Add binding information from Summary Catalogue.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
